steps.instruction: Tie a slip knot or alpine butterfly in the standing part of the rope.
steps.helpDetail: This forms the upper loop. Leave enough tail to go around the anchor and back up.

steps.instruction: Pass the free end around the anchor point (hook, tree, bumper).
steps.helpDetail: Go completely around the anchor point.

steps.instruction: Bring the free end up and through the upper loop.
steps.helpDetail: This creates a 3:1 mechanical advantage pulley system.

steps.instruction: Pull down hard to tension, then secure with two half hitches.
steps.helpDetail: Cinch it tight. Lock it in place so it doesn't slip. Essential for securing loads or guy lines.

techniques.name: Prusik Knot
techniques.subtitle: Friction hitch that slides and grips

steps.instruction: Take a smaller loop of cord (prussik loop) behind the main rope.
steps.helpDetail: The cord must be thinner than the main rope (e.g., 5mm cord on 10mm rope).

steps.instruction: Pass the knot end through the loop center 3 times, wrapping the main rope.
steps.helpDetail: Wrap it neatly inside itself. Keep coils flat and parallel.

steps.instruction: Pull the knot tight and dress the coils.
steps.helpDetail: Make sure the coils are not crossed. It should look symmetrical.

steps.instruction: Test grip: Pull on loop to lock, push barrel to slide.
steps.helpDetail: Used for ascending ropes or capturing progress in hauling systems.

techniques.name: Sheet Bend
techniques.subtitle: Join two ropes of different thicknesses

steps.instruction: Form a bight (U-shape) with the thicker rope.
steps.helpDetail: Hold the bight in one hand.

steps.instruction: Pass the thinner rope UP through the bight from below.
Wrap the thinner rope around the back of the thick bight.
steps.helpDetail: Go behind both strands of the thick rope.

steps.instruction: Tuck the thin rope under itself (across the top of the bight).
steps.helpDetail: Do not go back down the hole. Tuck it under its own standing part.

techniques.name: Alpine Butterfly Loop
techniques.subtitle: Strong fixed loop in the middle of a rope

steps.instruction: Wrap the rope twice around your hand to make 3 strands.
steps.helpDetail: Palm up. Strand 1 (closest to thumb), Strand 2, Strand 3 (fingertips).

steps.instruction: Take Strand 1 and move it to the center (between 2 and 3).
Take the NEW Strand closest to thumb (was 2) and pull it all the way across to fingertips.
steps.helpDetail: Move it over the other two.

steps.instruction: Tuck it underneath the other strands and pull through to form the loop.
steps.helpDetail: Pull the loop and the ends to tighten. Loads 3 ways perfectly.

techniques.name: Constrictor Knot
techniques.subtitle: Binding knot that will not slip

steps.instruction: Make a turn around the object, crossing the rope over itself.
steps.helpDetail: Form an X on the surface.

steps.instruction: Route the working end under the intersection of the X.
steps.helpDetail: Go over the standing part, then under the crossing point.

steps.instruction: Pull tight. The knot binds fiercely.
steps.helpDetail: Often requires a knife to cut off. Use for bag closures or temporary hose clamps.
